data.title: Piper Sandler Downgrades EPAM Systems to Neutral From Overweight, Adjusts PT to $170 From $251
data.description: EPAM Systems has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $242.50, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Singapore Equities Extend Gains Despite US Curbs on China-Bound Nvidia Chips
data.description:
Singapore shares surged at the close on Wednesday, bucking a downbeat trend among Asian peers caused by the Trump administration's order to tighten curbs on Nvidia's chip exports to China.

The Straits Times Index (STI), a key benchmark for the Singapore Exchange, ranged between 3,619.44 and 3,662.45 throughout the day. It ended the session at 3,662.45, up 37.73 points or 1.0% compared to Tuesday's close.

In company news, shares of China Everbright Water were up over 5% after it issued the 2025 second tranche of medium-term bonds worth 1 billion yuan with a maturity period of five years.

Oiltek International was up nearly 3% at the close after the company secured new contracts worth about 61.9 million ringgit from Africa, the Americas, Thailand, Indonesia and Malaysia.

Meanwhile, shares of Tianjin Pharmaceutical Da Ren Tang were down over 1% after the company proposed to dispose of its remaining 12% stake in Tianjin TSKF Pharmaceutical to Haleon China and Haleon CH.

data.title: Berenberg Lowers Barry Callebaut Price Target After 'Disappointing' Interim Performance
data.description:
Berenberg on Tuesday slashed its price target on Barry Callebaut Group to 1,160 francs from 1,650 francs after the Swiss chocolate producer posted a "disappointing" result for the fiscal first half and trimmed its full-year guidance.

The research firm maintained its buy rating on the stock.

Amid elevated cocoa prices and customer order phasing and its fiscal second-quarter volumes falling deeper than expected, Barry Callebaut guided for a mid-single-digit volume contraction for fiscal 2025, compared with its earlier forecast for a low-single-digit decline.

"The company still expects a double-digit increase in adjusted EBIT in constant FX, but also forecasts net finance expenses to reach CHF350m (previously CHF230m) due to higher hedging costs. We reduce our FY 2025 EPS forecasts by 30% - reflecting 4.4% volume declines, a 5% FX headwind, and a higher tax expense due to less favourable product mix," analysts wrote.

On the bright side, analysts expect the company's cost-plus model to provide some insulation from higher raw material costs. The research firm also said it expects the company's free cash flow to improve in the fiscal second half to result in a full-year outflow of 1.96 billion francs.

data.title: Raymond James Downgrades United Airlines to Market Perform From Outperform
data.description: United Airlines Holdings has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $97.93, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Most US Treasury Yields Rise Early Wednesday Ahead of Retail Sales, Industrial Production, Fed Chair Powell Speech
Tech Stocks Fall After U.S. Tightens Curbs on Nvidia's China Exports
data.description:
By Caitlin McCabe

The trade war is coming for two of the biggest names in the semiconductor industry--and that's unnerving markets.

Nvidia, the third-largest U.S. company by market value, warned late Tuesday that it will take a $5.5 billion charge after the U.S. government said it will need a license to export its H20 processors to China and other countries. These less-powerful AI chips had been designed to comply with American export controls.

Another industry linchpin also rattled investors. ASML, whose huge, costly machines are needed to make the most advanced chips, reported much weaker-than-expected orders and warned about tariff-related disruption.

Nvidia stock sank and ASML shares fell in Amsterdam. The moves rippled across global markets, with Nasdaq-100 futures losing more than 1%. Major indexes in Europe and Asia also declined.

Global stock markets started this week on a steadier note, after President Trump's tariff policies sparked haywire moves last week. Signs of anxiety re-emerged Wednesday: Wall Street's fear gauge, the Cboe Volatility Index, jumped after falling for three trading days. The dollar, meanwhile, extended its stretch of weakness.

On Tuesday, Trump said Beijing needs to make a trade deal with the U.S., because China needs American consumers and their spending.

Beijing named a new top trade negotiator, a former ambassador to the World Trade Organization. And new data Wednesday showed China's economy grew 5.4% in the first quarter from a year earlier, matching the pace of the previous quarter. Growth was partly driven by exports, which rocketed as U.S. importers raced to bring in orders ahead of anticipated new tariffs.

Later Wednesday, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is due to talk about the economic outlook. Data on retail sales is also due. That will measure activity in March, before Trump's tariff blitz.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Slovenia faces indirect hit from U.S. tariffs - c-bank
data.description:
LJUBLJANA (Slovenia), April 16 (SeeNews) - The reciprocal tariffs on imports announced by U.S. president Donald Trump's administration will indirectly affect Slovenia's economy and increase economic uncertainty, the Slovenian central bank said.

Although direct international trade between Slovenia and the U.S. is relatively small, the indirect effects of the introduction of U.S. tariffs through European markets and global value chains could be much greater, the central bank said in a report on Tuesday.

"Slovenia is a small and highly open economy, and increased protectionist tendencies could have a significant impact on it," the central bank said.

In February this year, the U.S. accounted for 2.4% of Slovenia's total exports and 1.1% of its total imports.

Around 15,000 jobs in Slovenia are connected to trade with the U.S., the central bank said.

In 2024, Slovenia's top exports to the U.S. in terms of value included nuclear reactors, boilers, machinery and mechanical appliances, electrical machinery and equipment, devices for recording or reproducing images and sound, optical, photographic, and medical instruments, as well as iron and steel.

Early this month, Trump unveiled a tariff of at least 10% on all exporters to the U.S., but paused its implementation for 90 days starting April 9. EU countries including Bulgaria, Romania, Slovenia and Croatia were set to face tariffs of 20%, applicable across the entire bloc.

data.title: Middle East Crude-Benchmarks gain for a third session
data.description:
Middle East crude benchmark spot premiums of Oman, Dubai and Murban edged up for a third consecutive session, as a sharp drop in oil prices buoyed demand from Asia.

A rising supply outlook and bleak demand expectation exacerbated by U.S.-China tariffs war have sent global oil prices plunging in recent weeks.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

Cash Dubai's premium to swaps rose 15 cents to $1.90 a barrel.

PetroChina will deliver a June-loading Murban crude cargo to TotalEnergies following the deals.

data.title: Allegro MicroSystems Rejects Onsemi's 'Inadequate' Takeover Offer
data.description:
Allegro MicroSystems said Wednesday that it had turned down acquisition offers from semiconductor rival Onsemi , calling the proposals 'inadequate' for its shareholders.

The company's statement follows Onsemi's recent announcement of withdrawing its unsolicited bid to buy Allegro.

The decision follows consultation with independent financial and legal advisors, the company said.

Allegro said it remained open to discussions and even laid out a path for constructive engagement with Onsemi, both before and after Onsemi made its proposal public, adding that Onsemi chose not to continue down that path.

Onsemi did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

data.title: Parkland Brief: Bob Espey, President & CEO, Has Announced Decision to Step Down
Swiss Kreis to launch buyout bid for Bosnia's Stocar
data.description:
SARAJEVO (Bosnia and Herzegovina), April 16 (SeeNews) - Switzerland's Kreis International said on Wednesday it plans to launch a takeover offer for the 75.6% stake in Bosnian meat producer Stocar [BANJ:STOR] it doesn't already own.

The bidder will offer 0.06 marka ($0.03/0.03 euro) per share for the 4,447,561 shares it does not own, Kreis said in a filing to the Banja Luka bourse.

Kreis said price is not based on recent market activity or legal valuation methods, because it hasn’t bought any shares in the past year and Stocar's shares are illiquid. In addition, Stocar is in financial trouble, with its losses exceeding its total capital.

(1 euro = 1.956 marka)

data.title: How Business Credit Reports Impact Loan Terms and Interest Rates
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 16, 2025 / If you are considering applying for a loan as a business owner, it needs to be stressed that a business credit report plays a huge role in determining your loan eligibility, terms and interest rates. So, if you aren't sure what goes on in your business credit report or how to improve it, read on for more information about how to monitor and improve this reflection of your business's financial state.

Understanding Your Business Credit Report

A business credit report is a record of your company's finances, including payment history with suppliers and lenders, credit inquiries, public legal filings such as bankruptcies or liens, and any outstanding debts. It demonstrates a company's financial stability, showing how you can manage debt and meet your financial obligations. Lenders may use this report to assess the likelihood of timely repayments and the overall risk of issuing credit.

Your business credit score is a number based on your business credit report and represents your risk level to lenders. Some of the major business credit score companies include Dun & Bradstreet, Experian, Equifax and FICO. All the agencies collect financial data about your business from banks, lenders and public records to analyze your payment history, credit utilization, outstanding debts, loan repayments, types of credit and any defaults or bankruptcies, and produce a score representing the financial health of your business. All the credit agencies use different score ranges, usually on a scale of 1 to 100, although some agencies, like Equifax, produce multiple types of scores that account for factors including the likelihood of default, bankruptcy and delinquency. For the FICO Small Business Scoring Service, the range is from 0 to 300, with at least a score of 140 being required to qualify for an SBA 7(a) loan.

How a Business Credit Report and Score Impact Loan Terms

A strong business credit scores can lead to better loan terms, including lower interest rates, especially for traditional business loans, SBA loans or business lines of credit. Lenders see businesses with excellent scores as a lower risk of defaulting on their payments. Higher credit scores can lead to more flexible repayment periods and loan structures.

On the other hand, a lower credit score can mean higher risk to lenders, which may lead to less preferable loan terms. Businesses with poor credit may deal with higher interest rates, shorter repayment periods and possible collateral requirements to secure funding.

However, these rates depend on the types of loans you want, the lender's policies and your credit score. If your business has a history of paying back loans on time, lenders may see you as a lower risk, and your interest rates can reflect that.

Ways to Improve Your Business Credit Report

Steps you can take to help improve your business credit report are firstly paying your bills on time- this includes invoices, loans, and credit card balances. Payment history is an essential component in determining your business credit score. Any missed or late payments can significantly damage it, and any defaults, liens or bankruptcies can stay on your credit history for seven to 10 years which can be very tough on your business's security.

Another tactic for improving your business credit report is reducing your Debt-to-Credit Ratio which entails lowering your outstanding debt relative to your available credit. Working with responsible vendors who report to credit bureaus is also a way to help your credit history. Taking care of any outstanding balances or debts can be good for your score as well.

Similar to a personal credit score, keeping old accounts open and diversifying your credit can have a positive impact. Additionally, make sure you regularly review your credit report for anything incorrect. If you find anything wrong, call up the credit bureau who reported the mistake and file a dispute.

Better Business Credit Scores for Better Loan Terms

Your business credit score plays an important role in whether you're able to secure a business loan and its terms. It's important to monitor your business credit report and report any errors, improve it, when possible, with on-time payments, and take the steps necessary to secure solid loan terms that will help your business succeed in the long run.

CONTACT:

Sonakshi Murze

Manager

sonakshi.murze@iquanti.com

SOURCE: iQuanti

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description e.g. call option

  • Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.

  • Number of securities to which option relates

  • Exercise price per unit

  • Type

    e.g. American, European etc.

  • Expiry date

  • Option money paid/ received per unit

(ii)        Exercise

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description

    e.g. call option

  • Exercising/ exercised against

  • Number of securities

  • Exercise price per unit

(d)        Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)

  • Class of relevant security

  • Nature of dealing

    e.g. subscription, conversion

  • Details

  • Price per unit (if applicable)

4.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the person making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • none

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the person making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • none

(c)        Attachments

  • Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?

  • NO

Date of disclosure:

16th April 2025

Contact name:

Tooshina Goorcharran

Telephone number*:

+356 2248 4406

Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.

The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

*If the discloser is a natural person, a telephone number does not need to be included, provided contact information has been provided to the Panel's Market Surveillance Unit.

The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk.

data.title: Finland's Tokmanni Set to Open New Discount Store Branch in Växjö, Sweden
data.description:
Tokmanni Group will open its new Dollarstore branch in Växjö, Sweden, on April 25, according to a Wednesday release.

The new 3,200-square-meter discount store replaces the Finnish retailer's soon-to-be-closed local branch. It is part of the group's strategy to expand and renew its store network in Sweden, Denmark and Finland.

data.title: Malaysian Shares End Lower Amid Lingering US Tariff Uncertainties
data.description:
Malaysian shares ended lower on Wednesday, weighed down by looming uncertainty over whether the next move in the US tariff dispute will escalate or ease the ongoing trade tensions.

The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI, the main gauge of Malaysian stocks, shed 9.51 points to end 0.64% lower at 1,476.92. The day range was between 1,472.84 and 1,487.50.

While US President Donald Trump temporarily suspended auto tariffs, he has also hinted at the potential new tariffs on the pharmaceutical and semiconductor sectors.

In corporate news, shares of Mayu Global Group dropped 3% after the company said its bank accounts holding about 10.6 million ringgit were frozen by Malaysian police as part of an ongoing investigation under anti-money laundering laws.

RHB Bank slid over 1% after it was named the third defendant in a lawsuit filed by Prismaworld Embassyview over the sale of a leasehold property in Kuala Lumpur.

data.title: EIA Expects U.S. Energy Consumption to Decrease Before Rising in Early 2040s
data.description:
The U.S. Energy Information Administration expects U.S. energy consumption to decrease before increasing in the early 2040s through 2050, according to its Annual Energy Outlook 2025 published on Tuesday.

U.S. energy consumption in 2050 is forecast to be lower than in 2024 in most of the scenarios explored by the EIA in its outlook.

However, the range of outcomes varies significantly based on the underlying assumptions. These include high oil prices, high or low oil and gas supply, high or low zero-carbon technology cost and high or low economic growth.

The outlook, which explores long-term U.S. energy trends, relies on a reference case that assumes laws and regulations in effect as of December 2024 remain in effect through 2050. It also includes scenario-based analyses of separate side cases that make various other assumptions about the energy sector.

The EIA said it made updates to the model that underpins the results, adding a hydrogen market module; a carbon capture, allocation, transportation and sequestration module; and an enhanced upstream oil and gas resources module.

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0938 GMT - China's 1Q GDP data shows a solid start to the year, but U.S. tariff concerns raise more downside risks to annual growth forecasts, Julius Baer economist Julian Schaerer writes in an email. China's outlook has darkened amid renewed trade tensions and persistent domestic challenges, he adds. Export momentum is expected to slow sharply in the months ahead, with U.S. levies on China now totaling up to 145% and Chinese tariffs on the U.S. at 125%, he says. Uncertainty around global demand and policy direction remains elevated, and more stimulus from China may be needed to stabilize sentiment and support momentum in coming months, he says. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0924 GMT - The cost of insuring euro-denominated credit against default using credit default swaps rises as sentiment deteriorates following news about U.S. restrictions on chip exports to China. The announcement deepens the uncertainty surrounding U.S. trade policies, lowering appetite for risk assets, Ricardo Evangelista, senior analyst at ActivTrades, says in a note. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index which tracks euro junk bond credit default swaps rises 6 basis points to 372bps, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. The iTraxx Europe Main index which tracks euro investment-grade CDS climbs 1 basis point to 73bps. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0923 GMT - China's central bank may frontload its monetary policy easing to stabilize markets, with the near-term prospect of a 100bps cut in banks' reserve requirement ratio, UOB economist Ho Woei Chen writes in a note. The economist also expects a 20bps cut in the 7-day reverse repo rate by the end of 2Q. China's economy expanded at a better-than-expected pace in 1Q, and its strong industrial sector growth is likely due to frontloading activities, Ho adds. Despite the robust 1Q GDP, UOB downgrades its full-year GDP growth forecast for China to 4.3% from 4.7%, citing the escalation of U.S.-China tariffs, she says.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0904 GMT - The euro pares gains against the dollar after data showed eurozone residents increased investments in non-eurozone equities. Residents' net purchases of non-eurozone equities in portfolio investments rose to 199 billion euros in the 12 months to February, compared to 134 billion euros in the year to January. However, non-residents' purchases of euro-area equities rose to 408 billion euros in the year to February, compared to 334 billion euros in January. The rotation towards European equities has contributed to the euro's recent gains. The euro trades up 0.6% at $1.1349 after the data, compared to $1.1387 beforehand. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0901 GMT - Investors prefer U.K. stocks over U.S. stocks, Bank of America's global fund managers survey for April shows. Investors also favor bonds, utilities and staples, while they are underweight the tech sector, the energy sector and industrials, according to the survey. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0849 GMT - Bitcoin edges lower after Nvidia said it faces a $5.5 billion hit from tighter U.S. rules on shipments of its new artificial intelligence chip to China. The microchip maker will require licences to export its chip to China as the U.S.-China trade war escalates. "Trump also launched a probe into whether critical minerals should face tariffs, so that added to fears that further tariffs were on the horizon," Deutsche Bank analysts say in a note. That raises fears that the trade war could still get worse from here, they say. Bitcoin falls 0.9% to $83,215, according to LSEG. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0848 GMT - Lower-than-expected U.K. inflation in March suggests the Bank of England can proceed with further interest-rate cuts later this year, Berenberg's Andrew Wishart says in a note. The headline annual inflatoin rate fell to 2.6% from 2.8% in February. Most encouragingly, services inflation eased to 4.7% from 5.0%. Concerns that the downward trend in services inflation could stop is a big reason why the BOE might have only cut rates once more this year, he says. "Strong pay growth, forward looking survey data and persistent inflation in some parts of the services sector continue to support that view." But weaker March inflation suggests there might be slightly less inflationary pressure in the system than previously assumed, he says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

0841 GMT - Investors' risk appetite drops in April, hitting its lowest level since May 2023, Bank of America's monthly global fund manager survey says. A net 46% of investors say they are taking lower than normal levels of risks, the survey finds. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0839 GMT - The Chinese economy is expected to get a boost from economic stimulus in the second half of the year, according to Bank of America's April global fund manager survey. In this month's survey, 63% of responding investors expect a boost, while 27% doesn't expect a lift from economic stimulus. On Wednesday, China reported GDP growth of 5.4% in the first quarter on the year, helped by the government's stimulus. This was above expectations of 5.2% growth in The Wall Street Journal's poll. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0835 GMT - Investors consider a trade war triggering a global recession to be the biggest 'tail risk,' according to 80% of those polled in Bank of America's April global fund manager survey. A 'tail risk' is a risk that is considered unlikely but one which would cause losses to investors if it happened. This 80% reading is the largest concentration for a 'tail risk' in 15 years, BofA says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0826 GMT - The theme of U.S. exceptionalism has peaked as President Trump's tariff plans raise concerns about weaker growth, Bank of America's April global fund manager survey shows. Some 73% of investors surveyed say U.S. exceptionalism has peaked. The survey also indicates that Trump's plans for tax cuts aren't expected to offset the economic impact of tariffs. A total of 63% of investors don't believe tax cuts will boost growth in the second half of the year. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0813 GMT - The Bank of England should maintain a cautious approach to interest rate cuts after Wednesday's U.K. inflation data but sterling still looks set to underperform the euro, MUFG Bank's Derek Halpenny says in a note. Inflation eased more than expected to 2.6% in March. However inflation could reaccelerate in the second quarter given the rise in employers' national insurance tax in April and increase in the energy price cap, he says. There's also uncertainty over the impact of U.S. tariffs on inflation. Sterling should continue to underperform the euro as the dollar weakens, although quicker-than-expected progress on a U.S.-U.K. trade deal would reduce that underperformance, he says. The euro rises 0.5% to 0.8572 pounds. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)
